---
title: "From BI to AI: Making Data-Driven Decisions with Agentic AI"
format: html
---

![](RiceBusiness-transparent-logo-sm.png){width=40%}

### Instructor

[Kerry Back](https://kerryback.com)<br> (<a href="mailto:kerry.e.back@rice.edu">kerry.e.back@rice.edu)</a><br>
J. Howard Creekmore Professor of Finance and Professor of Economics

### Course Description

This course covers how AI agents can be used for personal productivity, enterprise data analysis, and organizational decision-making.  It is organized in eight sessions over four weeks.

Learn the latest AI models and tools and how they can be used for data-driven decision-making.  The course covers the use of AI for data handling, data analysis, optimization, generating Excel, PowerPoint, and Word files, and personal productivity.  It also explains how to build AI agents, how to ensure data security, and how to augment AI with document retrieval.

The AI Lab provides each participant with access to Claude Code, currently the most popular AI coding tool.  Through the lab, participants get hands-on exposure to AI coding without needing to install any software or subscribe to an AI provider.

### Session Outline

**Session 1: The AI Landscape.** What AI can do today: chatbots, code agents, and the tools they produce.  Participants use AI to generate charts, Excel workbooks, PowerPoint decks, and Word documents from plain-English prompts.  Includes a primer on how large language models, chatbots, and agents work.

**Session 2: Data Wrangling.** Working with data across formats --- CSV, Excel, SQL databases, and Parquet files.  Participants use AI to query databases, merge and filter tables, and build visualizations.  Covers the difference between sandboxed chatbots and CLI agents that can access remote data sources.

**Session 3: Decision Making.** Using AI for optimization and multi-objective decision-making.  Participants use AI to assess current state, explore what-if scenarios, run optimization, and stress-test assumptions.

**Session 4: Building AI Agents.** How AI agents work under the hood: the agent loop of prompt, tool call, execute, and review.  Covers system prompts, tool definitions, and API calls.

**Session 5: Deployment and Governance.** Moving from prototype to production.  Covers three architecture options --- cloud API, on-premise, and hybrid --- with their cost, security, and compliance tradeoffs.  Includes a build-vs-buy evaluation framework and what production deployment requires: authentication, logging, error handling, and monitoring.

**Session 6: Small Language Models.** The cost and privacy tradeoff between frontier models via cloud APIs and small local models that run on-premise.  Covers when small models are sufficient, fine-tuning for domain-specific tasks, and hybrid architectures that route queries to different models based on complexity.

**Session 7: Predictive Modeling.** Using AI to build machine learning models for problems such as customer churn, revenue forecasting, and deal propensity.  Covers model evaluation, feature importance, and when to trust or distrust model output.

**Session 8: Capstone.** Participants present a scoped AI adoption strategy for their organization, synthesizing use cases, deployment approach, governance plan, and success metrics from across the course.
